template<typename T>
void add_eas_records(const std::string& table_name,
                     const std::vector<T>& records) {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  using NamedTupleType = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>;
  using Fields = typename NamedTupleType::Fields;

  // Collect column names, excluding auto-incrementing primary keys
  const auto columns = rfl::fields<Fields>() |
    transform([](const auto& f) -> std::optional<std::string> {
      using FieldType = std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(f)>;
      constexpr bool is_primary_key = HasPrimaryKey<FieldType>::value;
      constexpr bool is_auto_incr = HasAutoIncrPrimaryKey<FieldType>::value;
      if (is_primary_key && is_auto_incr) {
        return std::nullopt;
      } else {
        return f.name();
      }
    })) |
    filter([](const std::optional<std::string>& name) { return name; }) |
    transform([](auto range) {
      std::vector<std::string> res;
      for (auto& ptr : range) {
        res.push_back(*ptr);
      }
      return res;
    }));

  if (columns.empty()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("No columns to insert.");
  }

  const auto column_names = columns;
  const auto placeholders = rfl::get_field_placeholders<Fields>(false, true);

  // Construct the INSERT statement
  std::string insert_stmt = "INSERT INTO ";
  insert_stmt += table_name;
  insert_stmt += " (";
  insert_stmt += sqlgen::join(column_names, ", "));
  insert_stmt += ") VALUES (";
  insert_stmt += sqlgen::join(placeholders, ", "));
  insert_stmt += ");";

  // Connect to the database
  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);

  // Insert each record
  for (const auto& record : records) {
    const auto params = rfl::to_named_tuple(record);

    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>> bound_params;
    for (const auto& col : column_names) {
      auto it = params.find(col);
      if (it == params.end()) {
        bound_params.push_back(std::nullopt);
      } else {
        bound_params.push_back(sqlgen::to_sql_str(it->second));
      }
    }

    conn->execute(insert_stmt, bound_params);
  }
}

<answer>```cpp
// add+modify_eas_records.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <map>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

#include "eas_common.hpp"
using namespace sqlgen;
using namespace sqlgen::literals;

template <class T>
struct HasPrimaryKey;

template <class T>
struct HasPrimaryKey {
  constexpr static bool value = false;
};

template <class T, class PType>
struct HasPrimaryKey<sqlgen::PrimaryKey<T, PType>> {
  constexpr static bool value = true;
};

template <class T>
struct HasAutoIncrPrimaryKey;

template <class T>
struct HasAutoIncrPrimaryKey {
  constexpr static bool value = false;
};

template <class T, class PType>
  requires std::is_same_v<std::remove_cvref_t<PType>, sqlgen::auto_incr>)
struct HasAutoIncrPrimaryKey<sqlgen::PrimaryKey<T, PType>> {
  constexpr static bool value = true;
};

template <typename T>
void add_eas_records(const std::string& _table_name,
                     const std::vector<T>& _records) {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  using NamedTupleType = rfl::named_tuple_t<T>;
  using Fields = typename NamedTupleType::Fields;

  const auto columns = rfl::fields<Fields>() |
    transform([](const auto& _f) -> std::optional<std::string> {
      using FieldType = std::remove_cvref_t<decltype(_f)>;
      if constexpr (HasPrimaryKey<FieldType>::value &&
          HasAutoIncrPrimaryKey<FieldType>::value) {
        return std::nullopt;
      } else {
        return _f.name();
      }
    })) |
    filter([](const std::optional<std::string>& _name) { return _name; }) |
    transform([](auto&& _vec) {
      std::vector<std::string> res;
      for (auto& _ptr : _vec) {
        res.push_back(*_ptr);
      }
      return res;
    }));

  if (columns.empty()) {
    throw std::runtime_error("No columns to insert.");
  }

  const auto column_names = columns;
  const auto placeholders = rfl::get_field_placeholders<Fields>(false, true);

  const auto insert_stmt = "INSERT INTO " + _table_name + " ("
                           + sqlgen::join(column_names, ", "))
                           + ") VALUES ("
                           + sqlgen::join(placeholders, ", ")))
                           + ");";

  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);

  const auto prepared_stmt = conn->prepare(insert_stmt);

  for (const auto& _record : _records) {
    const auto params = rfl::to_named_tuple(_record);

    std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>> bound_params;
    for (const auto& _col : column_names) {
      const auto it = params.find(_col);
      bound_params.push_back(it != params.end()
           ? sqlgen::to_sql_str(it->second)
           : std::nullopt);
    }

    prepared_stmt->execute(bound_params);
  }
}

void modify_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id,
                              const std::string& _col_name,
                              const std::optional<std::string>& _new_value) {
  std::string primary_key_col;

  if (_table_name == "Student") {
    primary_key_col = "SNo";
  } else if (_table_name == "Course") {
    primary_key_col = "CID";
  } else if (_table_name == "Major") {
    primary_key_col = "MID";
  } else if (_table_name == "Score") {
    primary_key_col = "ScID";
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table.");
  }

  const auto set_clause = _col_name + " = " + (_new_value ? _new_value->str() : "NULL");
  const auto where_clause = primary_key_col + " = " + _record_id;

  const auto update_stmt = "UPDATE " + _table_name + " SET " + set_clause + " WHERE " + where_clause + ";";

  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);
  conn->execute(update_stmt);
}

void delete_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id) {
  std::string primary_key_col;

  if (_table_name == "Student") {
    primary_key_col = "SNo";
  } else if (_table_name == "Course") {
    primary_key_col = "CID";
  } else if (_table_name == "Major") {
    primary_key_col = "MID";
  } else if (_table_name == "Score") {
    primary_key_col = "ScID";
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table.");
  }

  const auto delete_stmt = "DELETE FROM " + _table_name + " WHERE " + primary_key_col + " = " + _record_id + ";";

  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path);
  conn->execute(delete_stmt);
}

  float price;

namespace test_create_eas_database {
  void create_eas_database() {
    const auto data_dir = std::filesystem::path("data");
    if (!std::filesystem::exists(data_dir)) {
      std::filesystem::create_directory(data_dir);
    }

    const auto main_db_path = "test_db.sqlite";
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(main_db_path).value();

    const auto attach_sql = "ATTACH DATABASE 'data/eas_database.db' AS EAS_DB;";
    conn->execute(attach_sql);
  }
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace delete_inactive_users {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> last_login;
  std::string status = "active";
};

int delete_inactive_users() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto session = conn->begin_transaction();

    const auto cutoff_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 1, 1);
    const auto query =
        sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where(
            [&](const auto& _user) { return _user.last_login < cutoff_date; }));

    const auto res = session->execute(query)
        .transform([&](const auto&) { session->commit(); return 0; })
        .or_else([&](const auto& _err) {
          session->rollback();
          return error(_err.what());
        });

    return res.value_or(-1);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return -1;
  }
}
}  // namespace delete_inactive_users
```</answer>

But I'm not sure if that's possible. Alternatively, perhaps the code can use the >= and <= operators with the dates set to the start and end of the day. For example, start_date is 2025-12-01 00:00:00, end_date is 2025-12-31 23:59:59. This would capture all times on those days. But the reference SQL's approach would include any time on those days. So perhaps this is the correct approach.

So in code:

auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 12, 1);
auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59);

Then, the WHERE clause is:

where(Post::created_at >= start_date && Post::created_at <= end_date)

This would include all posts created on or after 2025-12-01 00:00:00 and before 2025-12-31 23:59:59+1 second. Which covers the entire day of 2025-12-31.

But the reference SQL uses DATE(created_at) which truncates the time. So if the created_at in the database is stored as a datetime, then the code's approach is more precise. But if the created_at is stored as a date (without time), then the code's approach is the same as the reference SQL.

Assuming that the created_at is stored as a datetime, the code's approach is correct. So proceed with that.

Putting it all together:

The function query_active_users_posts will:

- Connect to the database.
- Create the User and Post tables.
- Execute a query joining User and Post, filtering by created_at between the dates.
- Return the distinct users as ActiveUser structs.

Now, implementing this in code.

First, the connection:

auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

Then, create the tables:

conn.create_table<User>();
conn.create_table<Post>();

Then, build the query:

auto query = conn.select_from<User, Post>(
    User::user_id, User::username,
    where(Post::created_at >= start_date && Post::created_at <= end_date)
)
.distinct()
.to<std::vector<ActiveUser>>();

But wait, the join between User and Post is on user_id, which is the primary key of User and foreign key of Post. So the join is correct.

But in sqlgen, when you call select_from with two tables, it joins them on their primary keys. So User and Post are joined on user_id. So that's correct.

Then, the where clause filters the posts created in the date range.

Then, distinct() ensures that each user is listed only once, even if they have multiple posts in the range.

Finally, convert the result to a vector of ActiveUser.

So putting all together:

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>
#include <sstream>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables
  conn.create_table<User>();
  conn.create_table<Post>();

  // Define date range
  const auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 12, 1);
  const auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime(2025, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59);

  // Execute query
  const auto query = conn.select_from<User, Post>(
      User::user_id, User::username,
      where(Post::created_at >= start_date && Post::created_at <= end_date)
  )
      .distinct()
      .to<std::vector<ActiveUser>>();

  return query.value_or(std::vector<ActiveUser>());
}
}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "query_course_enrollment_stats.hpp"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  const auto db_path = "test_db.sqlite";

  // Connect to the database.
  auto session = sqlgen::Session<sqlite3>::make(db_path);

  // Create tables if they don't exist.
  session->create_as<Course>();
  session->create_as<Enrollment>();

  // Prepare and execute the query.
  const auto query = session->prepare(
      "SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) AS student_count, AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade "
      "FROM Course c "
      "LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id "
      "GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;");
  const auto res = query.execute();

  // Process the results.
  std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
  for (const auto& row : res | transform([](auto&& _res) { return _res; })) {
    CourseStats cs;
    cs.title = row[0].visit(
        [](auto&& _v) -> std::string { return std::string(_v.str()); });
    cs.student_count = row[1].visit(
        [](auto _v) -> int { return static_cast<int>(_v); });
    cs.avg_grade = row[2].transform(
        [](auto _v) -> std::optional<double> {
          return std::optional<double>(_v);
        }));
    stats.emplace_back(std::move(cs));
  }

  return stats;
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create tables if they don't exist
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Customer>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Order>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<OrderItem>>());
  conn.execute(sqlgen::to_sql<CreateTable<Product>>());

  const std::string sql = R"(
    SELECT p.name, o.order_date, oi.quantity
    FROM Customer c
    JOIN "Order" o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN Product p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE c.customer_id = ?
    ORDER BY o.order_date DESC;
  )";

  auto prepared_stmt = conn.prepare_statement(sql);
  auto result = prepared_stmt.read(customer_id);

  std::vector<PurchaseHistory> history;
  if (result) {
    for (const auto& row : result.value()) {
      PurchaseHistory entry;
      entry.product_name = row[0].value_or("Unknown Product");
      entry.order_date = sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">::from_string(
          row[1].value_or("1970-01-01 00:00:00")));
      entry.quantity = std::stoi(row[2].value_or("0"));
      history.push_back(std::move(entry));
    }
  }

  return history;
}

}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history
```</answer>

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

struct PageVisit {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> visit_id;
  std::string url;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> visit_time;
};

struct DailyTraffic {
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

struct DailyTrafficView {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "DailyTrafficView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string visit_date;
  int total_visits;
  int unique_pages;
};

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<PageVisit>(
      sqlgen::col("visit_time").strftime("%Y-%m-%d").as("visit_date"),
      sqlgen::count().as("total_visits"),
      sqlgen::count(sqlgen::distinct(sqlgen::col("url"))).as("unique_pages"))
      .group_by("visit_date")
      .order_by("visit_date");
  return conn->template read<std::vector<DailyTraffic>>(query);
}
}  // namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "query_employee_hierarchy.hpp"

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  const auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = session.selectFrom<Employee>("e1")
      .leftJoin<Employee>("e2", "e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id")
      .select("e1.name", "e2.name");

  const auto res = session.execute(query);

  if (!res) {
    return {};
  }

  std::vector<EmployeeManager> result;
  for (const auto& row : res.value()) {
    result.emplace_back(
        .employee = row.get<std::string>("e1.name"),
        .manager = row.get<std::optional<std::string>>("e2.name"));
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy
```</answer>

import sqlgen
from datetime import datetime

class Customer(sqlgen.Model):
    tablename = 'Customer'
    customer_id = sqlgen.IntegerField(primary_key=True)
    name = sqlgen.StringField()

class Order(sqlgen.Model):
    tablename = 'Order'
    order_id = sqlgen.IntegerField(primary_key=True)
    customer_id = sqlgen.IntegerField()
    total_amount = sqlgen.FloatField()

import sqlgen

    total_amount = sqlgen.FloatField()
    order_date = sqlgen.DateTimeField()

            .execute()
        )

#include <ranges>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace query_product_sales_report {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string category;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct CategoryReport {
  std::string category;
  int total_qty;
  double total_amt;
};

std::vector<CategoryReport> query_product_sales_report() {
  const auto sql = R"(
    SELECT p.category, SUM(s.quantity) AS total_qty, SUM(s.amount) AS total_amt
    FROM Product p
    INNER JOIN Sale s ON p.product_id = s.product_id
    GROUP BY p.category
    HAVING total_amt > 5000
  )";

  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto session = conn.value()->begin_transaction();

  const auto res = session->execute(sql);

  if (!res) {
    // Handle error.
    return {};
  }

  const auto to_category_report = [&](const auto& row) {
    return CategoryReport{.category = row.get<Names::category>(),
                          .total_qty = row.get<Names::total_qty>(),
                          .total_amt = row.get<Names::total_amt>()};
  };

  using namespace std::ranges::views;
  return res->transform([](auto range) {
    return std::vector<CategoryReport>(range |
                                          transform(to_category_report));
  }).value_or({});
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "query_search_books_by_criteria.hpp"

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& _filters) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>().where(
      [&_filters](const auto& _book) -> bool {
        return (!(_filters.title_keyword) || _book.title.str().find(*_filters.title_keyword) != std::string::npos) &&
               (!(_filters.min_year) || _book.year >= *_filters.min_year) &&
               (!(_filters.genre) || _book.genre == *_filters.genre) &&
               (!(_filters.max_price) || _book.price <= *_filters.max_price);
      });

  return conn->execute(query).transform(
      [](auto&& _stream) { return sqlgen::to_vector<Book>(_stream); })
      .value_or(std::vector<Book>());
}
}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```</answer>

So the final code would be:

#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_manage_indexes {

  struct Product {
    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
    std::string sku;
    std::string name;
    std::string category;
    double price;
  };

  bool manage_indexes() {
    try {
      auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
      
      // Create the Product table
      auto create_table_stmt = sqlgen::write_to_create_table<Product>();
      conn->execute(create_table_stmt.str());
      
      // Create indexes with IF NOT EXISTS
      const std::string idx_category_sql = "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_category ON Product(category);";
      const std::string idx_sku_sql = "CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_sku ON Product(sku);";
      const std::string idx_name_price_sql = "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_product_name_price ON Product(name, price);";
      
      conn->execute(idx_category_sql);
      conn->execute(idx_sku_sql);
      conn->execute(idx_name_price_sql);
      
      return true;
    } catch (const std::exception& e) {
      // Log the error if needed
      return false;
    }
  }

}  // namespace schema_manage_indexes

This should fulfill all the requirements. The function connects to the database, creates the table, creates the required indexes with the IF NOT EXISTS logic, and returns a boolean indicating success.

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_connection("test_db.sqlite");

    // Check if the view already exists
    auto check_stmt = "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='view' AND name='SalesSummary';";
    auto check_res = conn->execute(check_stmt);
    auto check_rows = check_res->read_all<std::vector<std::map<std::string, std::optional<std::string>>>>();

    if (!check_rows.empty()) {
      // Drop existing view
      auto drop_stmt = "DROP VIEW SalesSummary;";
      if (!conn->execute(drop_stmt)) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    // Construct the SELECT query for the view
    auto fields = sqlgen::fields(
        "p.name AS product_name",
        "SUM(s.quantity) AS total_quantity",
        "SUM(s.amount) AS total_revenue");

    auto from = sqlgen::from<Sale, Product>(
        sqlgen::JoinType::inner_join,
        "s",
        "p",
        sqlgen::On<std::equal_to<Sale::product_id, Product::product_id>>());

    auto where = Nothing{};
    auto group_by = sqlgen::group_by("p.name");

    auto select_query = sqlgen::select_from(from, fields, where, group_by);

    // Create the view
    const auto create_view_sql = "CREATE VIEW SalesSummary AS " + select_query.str();
    return conn->execute(create_view_sql);
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace right_join_departments_projects {

struct Department {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Project {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> project_id;
  int department_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct DepProj {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::string project_name;
};

std::vector<DepProj> right_join_departments_projects() {
  auto pool = sqlgen::ConnectionPool<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>::make("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = pool->getSession();

  const auto query = session->selectFrom<Project>("p")
      .join<Department>(sqlgen::JoinType::Right, "d", "p.department_id = d.department_id")
      .fields(d_.name().as("department_name"), p_.name().as("project_name"));

  return query.transform([](auto range) {
    return sqlgen::read<DepProj>(std::move(range));
  }).value_or(std::vector<DepProj>());
}
}  // namespace right_join_departments_projects
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto a = sqlgen::make_table<A>();
  auto b = sqlgen::make_table<B>();

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from(a)
      .fields(a.name, b.score)
      .join(b, "a.id = b.a_id"_p)
      .where(b.score > 10 && a.name.like("%x%"))
      .order_by(b.score.desc())
      .limit(5)
      .to_sql();

  return query.str();
}

}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

// ... structs Event and TemporalAnalysis ...

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::connect<sqlgen::sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");

      "SELECT event_id, event_name, DAY(start_time), MONTH(start_time), YEAR(start_time), "
      "HOUR(start_time), MINUTE(start_time), SECOND(start_time), WEEKDAY(start_time), "
      "UNIXEPOCH(start_time), DAYS_BETWEEN(start_time, end_time) "
      "FROM Event "
      "WHERE end_time IS NOT NULL"
  );

}

    # Connect to database
    conn = sqlgen.connect(SQLite("test_db.sqlite"))

    User.create_as(conn, if_not_exists=True)

    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id
      AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u2.user_id
      )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;
    """
    result1 = conn.execute(query1).fetchall()
    
    # Execute query 2: users with more than N friends (using average)

    SELECT u.user_id, u.username, COUNT(f.user_id2) as friend_count

    """

    # Execute query3: friend-of-friend (exactly one mutual friend)

    SELECT u1.username, u3.username

    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u3 ON f2.user_id2 = u3.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u3.user_id
      AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3

      )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u3.user_id

    """
    result3 = conn.execute(query3).fetchall()
    
    # Execute query4: count friends per user and identify most connected

import sqlgen
from sqlgen import SQLite

class User(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Text, unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Text, unique=True, nullable=False)
    join_date = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.DateTime, nullable=False)
    city = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Text, nullable=True)

class Friendship(sqlgen.Table):
    user_id1 = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, foreign_key="User.user_id", primary_key=True)
    user_id2 = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.Integer, foreign_key="User.user_id", primary_key=True)
    friendship_date = sqlgen.Column(sqlgen.DateTime, nullable=False)

def analyze_social_network():
    conn = sqlgen.connect(SQLite("test_db.sqlite"))
    User.create_as(conn, if_not_exists=True)
    Friendship.create_as(conn, if_not_exists=True)
    
    # Query 1: Mutual friends (friends who share the same friend)
    query1 = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username, COUNT(f1.user_id2) as mutual_friends
    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id
      AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u2.user_id
      )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;
    """
    result1 = conn.execute(query1).fetchall()
    
    # Query 2: Users with more than average friends
    query2 = """
    SELECT u.user_id, u.username, COUNT(f.user_id2) as friend_count
    FROM User u
    LEFT JOIN Friendship f ON u.user_id = f.user_id1
    GROUP BY u.user_id, u.username
    HAVING friend_count > (
      SELECT AVG(friend_count) 
      FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) as friend_count FROM Friendship GROUP BY user_id1)
    )
    ORDER BY friend_count DESC;
    """
    result2 = conn.execute(query2).fetchall()
    
    # Query3: Friend-of-friend (exactly one mutual friend)
    query3 = """
    SELECT u1.username, u3.username
    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u3 ON f2.user_id2 = u3.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u3.user_id
      AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u3.user_id
      )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u3.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(f2.user_id1) = 1;
    """
    result3 = conn.execute(query3).fetchall()
    
    # Query4: Most connected users
    query4 = """
    SELECT u.user_id, u.username, COUNT(f.user_id2) as friend_count
    FROM User u
    LEFT JOIN Friendship f ON u.user_id = f.user_id1
    GROUP BY u.user_id, u.username
    ORDER BY friend_count DESC;
    """
    result4 = conn.execute(query4).fetchall()
    
    # Query5: Same city, not friends
    query5 = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f 
        WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
           OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    );
    """
    result5 = conn.execute(query5).fetchall()
    
    return [result1, result2, result3, result4, result5]

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/connect.hpp"

namespace trim_and_length_validation {

struct UserProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct ValidatedProfile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string trimmed_name;
  std::string trimmed_email;
  int name_length;
  int email_length;
};

std::vector<ValidatedProfile> trim_and_length_validation() {
  const auto trim = [](const std::string& _str) {
    const auto start = _str.find_first_not_of(' ');
    if (start == std::string::npos) {
      return "";
    }
    const auto end = _str.find_last_not_of(' ');
    return _str.substr(start, end - start + 1);
  };

  const auto length = [](const std::string& _str) { return _str.size(); };

  using Names = rfl::named_tuple_t<UserProfile, false>;
  using ViewType =
      rfl::NamedTuple<rfl::Field<"user_id", int>,
                      rfl::Field<"trimmed_name", std::string>,
                      rfl::Field<"trimmed_email", std::string>,
                      rfl::Field<"name_length", int>,
                      rfl::Field<"email_length", int>>;

  const auto to_view = [&](const Names& _name) -> ViewType {
    return ViewType{
        .user_id = _name.user_id,
        .trimmed_name = trim(_name.name),
        .trimmed_email = trim(_name.email),
        .name_length = length(_name.name),
        .email_length = length(_name.email)};
  };

  const auto db = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = db->select_from<UserProfile>();
  const auto views = sqlgen::internal::collect::vector(
      query | transform(to_view));
  return rfl::from_named_tuple<std::vector<ValidatedProfile>>(std::move(views));
}
}  // namespace trim_and_length_validation
```</answer>

          order_id INT NOT NULL,
          product_id INT NOT NULL,

          quantity INT NOT NULL,

        order_id,
        product_id,
        unit_price,
        quantity,
        COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0) as discount_rate,
        COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0) as tax_rate,
        (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100)) as discounted_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0)/100)) as final_total,
        (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
        (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
        (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
      FROM OrderItem;
  )";

  auto res = conn->execute(query);

  // Fetch results into OrderMetrics
  std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics;
  for (auto& row : res) {
    OrderMetrics m;
    m.item_id = row.get<int>("item_id");
    m.order_id = row.get<int>("order_id");
    m.product_id = row.get<int>("product_id");

    m.discounted_total = row.get<double>("discounted_total");
    m.final_total = row.get<double>("final_total");
    m.unit_cost = row.get<double>("unit_cost");
    m.quantity_group = row.get<int>("quantity_group");
    m.estimated_profit = row.get<double>("estimated_profit");
    metrics.push_back(std::move(m));
  }

  return metrics;
}

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

<answer>

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

struct OrderMetrics {
  int item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;
  double final_total;
  double unit_cost;
  int quantity_group;
  double estimated_profit;
};

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(const std::vector<OrderItem>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect(":memory:");

  // Create the OrderItem table
  conn->execute(
      "CREATE TABLE OrderItem (
          item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
          order_id INT NOT NULL,
          product_id INT NOT NULL,
          unit_price REAL NOT NULL,
          quantity INT NOT NULL,
          discount_rate REAL,
          tax_rate REAL
      );"
  );

  // Insert all items into the table
  for (const auto& item : items) {
    conn->insert("OrderItem", item);
  }

  // Execute the SELECT query with COALESCE for discount_rate and tax_rate
  const auto query = R"(
      SELECT 
        item_id,
        order_id,
        product_id,
        unit_price,
        quantity,
        COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0) as discount_rate,
        COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0) as tax_rate,
        (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100)) as discounted_total,
        (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0)/100)) as final_total,
        (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
        (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
        (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
      FROM OrderItem;
  )";

  auto res = conn->execute(query);

  // Fetch results into OrderMetrics
  std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics;
  for (auto& row : res) {
    OrderMetrics m;
    m.item_id = row.get<int>("item_id");
    m.order_id = row.get<int>("order_id");
    m.product_id = row.get<int>("product_id");
    m.unit_price = row.get<double>("unit_price");
    m.quantity = row.get<int>("quantity");
    m.discount_rate = row.get<double>("discount_rate");
    m.tax_rate = row.get<double>("tax_rate");
    m.gross_total = row.get<double>("gross_total");
    m.discounted_total = row.get<double>("discounted_total");
    m.final_total = row.get<double>("final_total");
    m.unit_cost = row.get<double>("unit_cost");
    m.quantity_group = row.get<int>("quantity_group");
    m.estimated_profit = row.get<double>("estimated_profit");
    metrics.push_back(std::move(m));
  }

  return metrics;
}

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics
```

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace join_orders_customers_payments {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  int customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
};

struct Payment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> payment_id;
  int order_id;
  double amount;
};

struct Consolidated {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer_name;
  double amount;
};

std::vector<Consolidated> join_orders_customers_payments() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = conn->make_session();

  auto query = session.selectFrom<Customer, Order, Payment>(
      .on<Customer, Order>(Customer.customer_id == Order.customer_id)
      .on<Order, Payment>(Order.order_id == Payment.order_id)
      .order_by<Payment>(Payment.amount.desc())
      .transform([](auto range) {
        std::vector<Consolidated> vec;
        for (auto& res : range) {
          auto customer = res.get<Customer>();
          auto order = res.get<Order>();
          auto payment = res.get<Payment>();
          vec.emplace_back(
              .order_id = order.order_id,
              .customer_name = customer.name,
              .amount = payment.amount);
        }
        return vec;
      }));

  return query.value();
}
}  // namespace join_orders_customers_payments
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  int note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Update the content by replacing 'foo' with 'bar'
  conn->execute("UPDATE Notes SET content = REPLACE(content, 'foo', 'bar')");
  
  // Fetch all updated notes
  const auto notes = conn->template read<std::vector<Note>>(sqlgen::select_from<Note>());
  
  // Convert to UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> result;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    result.emplace_back(UpdatedNote{
        .note_id = note.note_id.value(),
        .updated_content = note.content});
  }
  return result;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```
</answer>

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  try {
    conn->begin_transaction();
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      conn->insert(item);
    }
    conn->commit();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    conn->rollback();
    throw;
  }

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT item_id, name, quantity, location 
    FROM Inventory 
    WHERE quantity > 50 
    ORDER BY quantity DESC
  )";

  const auto select_res = conn->execute(query);

  return select_res->size() > 0;
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
</answer>
output.result:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::SQLite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  try {
    conn->begin_transaction();
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      conn->insert(item);
    }
    conn->commit();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    conn->rollback();
    throw;
  }

  const auto query = R"(
    SELECT item_id, name, quantity, location 
    FROM Inventory 
    WHERE quantity > 50 
    ORDER BY quantity DESC
  )";

  const auto select_res = conn->execute(query);

  return select_res->size() > 0;
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>Okay, I need to write a function called cascade_delete_demo using the sqlgen library to demonstrate cascade deletion. Let's start by understanding the requirements.

First, the function should connect to an SQLite database named "test_db.sqlite". Then, create Parent and Child tables with the specified constraints. #pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = conn->select_from<Profile, Result>()
      .where(Profile::bio.is_null() || Profile::avatar_url.is_null());
  return query.value_or(std::vector<Result>());
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = conn.make_session();

    auto query = session.select_from<Supplier, Product>(
        sqlgen::Join<Supplier, Product, sqlgen::JoinType::FullOuterJoin,
                   [&](const auto& s, const auto& p) { return s.supplier_id == p.supplier_id; })
            .select([](const auto& _s, const auto& _p) {
              return std::make_pair(_s.name.str(), _p.name ? _p.name->str() : std::nullopt);
            })
    );

    return query.transform([](auto&& _vec) {
      std::vector<SupProd> result;
      for (const auto& [supplier_name, product_name] : _vec) {
        result.emplace_back(supplier_name, product_name);
      }
      return result;
    }).value_or(std::vector<SupProd>());
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;
  std::string status;

inline std::string delete_with_complex_condition() {

  auto query = sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where(condition);
  return query.str();
}
}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition

The function starts by connecting to the database. Then begins a transaction. Prepares the update statement. Loops through each product, executing the update with the parameters. If any execution fails, returns false. Otherwise, commits the transaction.

So the final code would look like this:

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  // Assuming the table already exists, so remove create_table<Product>(conn_res);

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();

  // Prepare the update statement once
  auto update_stmt = txn->prepare_statement("UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?");

  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    auto exec_res = update_stmt->execute(qty, pid);
    if (!exec_res) {
      return false;
    }
  }

  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    
    const std::string create_view_sql =
        "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
        "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS total "
        "FROM Order o "
        "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;";
    conn->execute(create_view_sql);
    
    auto results = conn->template select_from<OrderSummary>();
    return results;
}
}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

  std::string message;

}  // namespace drop_table_if_exists

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sales>(
      conn,
      sqlgen::columns<Sales>::sale_time.strftime("%w").as("weekday"),
      sqlgen::columns<Sales>::quantity.sum().as("total_quantity")
  )
      .group_by("weekday")
      .order_by("weekday");

  return query.to<std::vector<WeekdayStats>>();
}
}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

inline std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
  return sqlgen::update<Player>()
      .set(Player::score, sqlgen::columns().score + 10)
      .to_sql();
}
}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace group_by_with_aggregations {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct Stats {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string name;
  int post_count;
  std::optional<double> avg_length;
};

std::vector<Stats> group_by_with_aggregations() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = conn->prepare_statement(
      "SELECT u.user_id, u.name, COUNT(p.post_id) AS post_count, AVG(LENGTH(p.content)) AS avg_length "
      "FROM User u "
      "JOIN Post p ON u.user_id = p.user_id "
      "GROUP BY u.user_id, u.name;");
  std::vector<Stats> result;
  query->read(result);
  return result;
}
}  // namespace group_by_with_aggregations
```
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/transpilation.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace create_table_as_select {

struct Source {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int value;
};

struct Target {
  int value;
};

inline auto create_table_as_select() {
  using namespace sqlgen::transpilation;

  auto fields = make_fields<Source>(field<&Source::value>().as("value"));
  auto where = where<Source>(col("value") > 100);

  auto select_from = SelectFrom<Source, Nothing, Nothing, WhereType, FieldsType, AliasType>{
      .fields = fields,
      .alias = "s",
      .where = where};

  auto create_as = create_as<Nothing, TableWrapper<Target>, SelectFrom<Source, WhereType, FieldsType, AliasType>>(
      select_from,
      TableWrapper<Target>{},
      dynamic::CreateAs::What::table,
      false,
      false);

  return to_sql(std::move(create_as));
}

}  // namespace create_table_as_select
